Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

events.agenda: 5:00-5:25 Check in/Registration; 5:25-5:30 Welcome Remarks; 5:30-6:00 Speaker 1 + Q&A; 6:00-6:30 Speaker 2 + Q&A; 6:30-6:45 Closing Remarks; 6:45-7:00 Freeform Jazz.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.
events.location: 11520 W Pico Blvd, Los Angeles, CA
events.raw_text:
Mon, Mar 20, 2023 · 5:00 PM to 7:00 PM PDT

Presentation 1: Data products need a go-to-market strategy, using HourWork's data team GTM strategy as an example - Zack Ober (Analytics Lead, HourWork)
Presentation 2: How open-source data-diff can automate testing for dbt model changes - Kyle McNair (Data Engineer, Datafold)

talks.description: Data products, like every product, need a go-to-market (GTM) strategy; explores why a GTM strategy solves many challenges data teams face, using HourWork's data team GTM strategy as an example.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Presentation Description: Data products, like every product, need a go-to-market (GTM) strategy. Without one, your customers (stakeholders) won't understand how your products will benefit them.
talks.title: Data products need a go-to-market strategy

talks.description: How open-source data-diff automates testing to check whether code changes had unintended effects on data, speeding up development.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Presentation Description: When building dbt models, manual testing is still the most common way to check whether a change to your code had any unintended effects on the data. This process can slow down development and make you less likely to make changes in the future. Join us to learn how open-source data-diff can automate these tests.
talks.title: Automating dbt model testing with data-diff
